_meta.generator: manual-smoke (Stage 1 round-26: 大学 R25 base + cyclist 姿勢明示 (前傾 / 両手ハンドル / 両足ペダル on / dynamic motion / 7-head proportion))
_meta.vocabTypesSource: data/master_image_registry.json (word_building 4 entries; R26 は 大学 のみ。学校 R25 採用候補・デパート R22 + 会社 R22 採用確定で再生成不要)
_meta.notes: Stage 1 round-26: 大学 R25 で cyclist が直立 + 足がペダルに乗っていない + 体傾きなし = 静的な「自転車に座っているだけ」のような不自然な姿勢で user NG 評価。R26 = R25 ベース + cyclist のみ修正: (1) torso leans slightly forward 明示 + (2) BOTH hands clearly grip handlebars 明示 + (3) BOTH feet on pedals with knees bent in mid-pedaling rotation 明示 + (4) figure in clear motion NOT static pose 明示 + (5) image_3 (大学生) proportion anchor 強化。他の R25 改善 (全樹木 leafy summer 統一 / cyclist 服 warm yellow 禁止 / blank text rule / campus 副建物 OK) は維持。学校 R25 は user 採用候補 (本番化保留)。1 件 / ~$0.04 / cap 2/62 → 3/62。

vocabulary.prompt:
Flat vector illustration in a minimalist Japanese textbook style. Color palette is strictly limited to the muted pastel family — dull blues, sand-beiges, soft warm yellows, and soft greys — on a soft cream off-white background. Use no more than 3-4 distinct facade colors per image beyond the background and the outline ink. Lighting: soft and even, no harsh shadows. Tone: warm, friendly, calm, and inviting — never cold or mechanical.

View (CRITICAL — match image_5 architectural anchor): a street-level low-angle three-quarter perspective. The camera is positioned just outside the campus gate, at adult eye height, slightly looking up at the gate and the main academic facade behind it. The CAMPUS GATE (stone pillars + horizontal sign beam) and the MAIN ACADEMIC BUILDING FACADE sit near the center of the frame. This is NOT a top-down isometric overview and NOT a flat front-only elevation; it is a near, eye-level CLOSE-UP three-quarter view like image_5 (the existing 病院 hospital vocab card).

FRAMING (CRITICAL): close-up framing. The campus gate and the main academic building's central facade fill the frame substantially. The building's visible mass occupies the upper 75-85% of the canvas area, with the top edge of the roofline or building reaching close to the upper 10% of the canvas. The campus gate pillars and the building's side wings MAY extend beyond the left and right canvas edges (off-frame is encouraged). Do NOT center the entire building small in the canvas with large empty cream space around it.

CAMPUS CONTEXT: this is a university CAMPUS, not an isolated freestanding building. It is natural and encouraged for one or two additional simplified academic building silhouettes to appear in the middle ground or background behind the main building (smaller in scale, partially occluded by the main facade, drawn in the same cream/slate-grey palette). These are part of the campus context. Do NOT add commercial buildings, residential houses, or any non-academic structures; only secondary academic buildings are allowed in the background.

GROUND / PAVEMENT (CRITICAL — match image_5): the ground / sidewalk / pavement area at the bottom of the image must remain soft cream off-white, matching the background color exactly. Do NOT render the ground or sidewalk as slate-grey, blue-grey, or any darker color. The pavement should be indicated by subtle slate-navy outlines only (a single horizontal line marking the curb edge, outlined panels for any visible paving), with the interior fill of the pavement remaining the same cream off-white as the background.

Subject: A Japanese university campus entrance — a substantial stone campus gate (two stone pillars supporting a horizontal sign beam) standing in front of a stately academic building (four to five stories visible, with regular rows of rectangular windows). Signature feature: the stone campus gate with its horizontal sign beam. Per-building palette (strictly enforce): cream / off-white facade walls for the academic building (NOT brick-red, NOT terracotta — facade is light, almost matching the background), sand-stone beige as the single accent color used on the stone gate pillars, sign beam, and entrance trim, slate-grey for the roof and any structural detail. No other facade colors.

SIGNBOARD: the horizontal sign beam between the stone gate pillars reads "UNIVERSITY" in a clear, readable font. The sign beam is clearly readable and sized to be naturally legible at the gate.

BLANK TEXT SURFACES (CRITICAL — match image_5): the UNIVERSITY sign beam between the stone gate pillars is the ONLY text-bearing element anywhere in the image. Do NOT add other signboards, plaques, billboards, banners, name plates, address plates, bulletin boards, posters, or any other text-bearing surfaces anywhere on the gate, building, perimeter wall, sidewalk, scene, or background academic buildings. All other plaques, walls, gates, doors, windows, and surfaces MUST be LEFT BLANK — no text, no characters, no inscriptions, no symbols that resemble letters, no faux-Latin or faux-cursive squiggles. The single UNIVERSITY sign beam is the unique text element in the entire image.

People in scene (CRITICAL — variety of activities, at PROMINENT foreground scale roughly 1/3 of visible building height): four or five simplified figures animating the scene, each engaged in a different activity to reflect campus everyday life:
  - one university student entering through the gate, walking AWAY from the viewer toward the academic building (back of figure visible).
  - two university students walking past the gate along the sidewalk in mid-stride, side view, carrying backpacks or shoulder bags.
  - ONE UNIVERSITY STUDENT RIDING A BICYCLE ON THE SIDEWALK IN THE FOREGROUND, drawn in DYNAMIC SIDE-VIEW PROFILE in clear motion — NOT a static seated pose. CRITICAL cyclist pose requirements: (a) the cyclist's TORSO LEANS SLIGHTLY FORWARD over the handlebars in a natural riding posture (NOT sitting bolt upright). (b) BOTH HANDS clearly grip the handlebars (NOT hanging at the sides, NOT loose). (c) BOTH FEET are firmly on the pedals in MID-PEDALING ROTATION — one pedal is at the bottom of its stroke with the leg extended downward, the other pedal is at the top with the knee bent forward (the classic active-pedaling pose). Feet must NOT touch the ground; the bicycle is in motion. (d) the BICYCLE FRAME is sized correctly for the rider — when seated, the rider's extended leg nearly reaches full extension at the bottom of the pedal stroke (do not draw an undersized clown-bike or oversized frame). (e) the cyclist's BODY PROPORTIONS are well-balanced (7-head adult-young-adult proportion, matching image_3 (大学生 university-student card) — not childlike, not stubby). (f) optionally a slight motion line or hair/clothing sway suggests forward movement. The CYCLIST WEARS muted blue, soft grey, or sand-beige clothing — NOT warm yellow (warm yellow is reserved for accent surfaces and would clash with the building palette). Drawn at prominent foreground scale (NOT middleground, NOT shrunken).
  - optionally one pair of two university students standing near the gate engaged in casual conversation.
University students wear casual contemporary clothes (jeans, t-shirts, sweaters, light jackets — NOT school uniforms). Figures are drawn at adult-young-adult scale (7-head proportion), at the prominent foreground size specified above. Variety of postures and activities is more important than identical poses.

Landscaping: one or two simplified tree silhouettes near the gate and along the campus walkway (may be cropped at canvas edges per framing rule). ALL tree silhouettes in the image must be drawn consistently — all as simplified leafy summer trees with rounded foliage masses. Do NOT mix winter bare-branch trees with leafy summer trees in the same image; every tree is leafy summer. Foreground: the sidewalk and campus walkway lead through the gate toward the main academic building entrance.

Reference image guidance:
- image_1 (日本人 Japanese person card) anchors the overall brand voice — illustration style, line weight, palette tokens, face style.
- image_2 (学生 student card), image_3 (大学生 university-student card), and image_4 (先生 teacher card) are the references for the figures in the scene: match their head-to-body proportion, casual outfit rendering, line weight, and palette tokens. The cyclist and walking figures should be drawn as figures from the same vocabulary card series as image_3 (大学生) primarily — the cyclist's body proportion, posture quality, and rendering should match image_3's natural-figure standard.
- image_5 (existing 病院 hospital vocab card) is the architectural, compositional, FRAMING, GROUND-COLOR, and CLOSE-UP three-quarter perspective reference. CRITICAL: image_5 demonstrates EXACTLY the visual qualities we want for the university campus: (a) a close-up street-level low-angle three-quarter view, (b) the building mass extending toward the canvas edges, (c) cream off-white ground matching the background, (d) a restrained 3-color facade palette (light walls + single accent + slate roof/structural grey), (e) figures at prominent foreground scale. Replicate these qualities for the university image. Do not copy image_5's subject (this is a university campus, not a hospital) — only echo its DEPICTION STYLE: framing, perspective, scale, ground color, and palette discipline.

Defined deep slate-navy outlines, no gradients, no shadows, no 3D effects. 1:1 square aspect ratio. Minimalist and elegant overall feel.
